summary: Closed/unmerged PR centered on vi0102 decomp work, with the author explicitly calling out a reusable vi-file naming convention, prototype cleanup, and near matches limited mainly by register swaps and allocation differences. The diff also contains a large MetroTRK asm-to-C migration and build-tooling/compiler updates, but the PR body does not explain that part, so its intent is inferred from changed files rather than review discussion.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/vi/vi0102.c from stubs/unknowns into typed code for Vi0102 scene setup, including archive data structs, camera, joint, fog, light, player, item, stage, audio, and effect initialization calls.

Renamed vi0102 functions from address-style names such as vi_8031CC68 and vi_8031CC8C to more descriptive/reusable names such as vi0102_JObjCallback, vi0102_CameraCallback, vi0102_RunFrame, and vi0102_Initialize.
Changed vi0102.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed function signatures, including vi0102_8031CB00(s8, s8), vi0102_Initialize(un_804D6F60_t*), and callback signatures.
Refined un_804D6F60_t from two inferred s8 fields plus broad padding into four inferred s8 fields plus padding, while preserving STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(un_804D6F60_t) == 0x8).
Renamed the inline helper in src/melee/vi/vi.h from vi_EraseScreen to vi_RunCamera, reflecting that it sets current camera, erase color, viewport, projection, and scissor before drawing.
Updated vi0501 camera callback usage to match the revised convention: GXColor erase_colors_vi0501, callback signature with an unused int, and vi_RunCamera((u8*) &erase_colors_vi0501, ...).
Improved Player_80036E20 prototype and implementation by naming its first parameter CharacterKind ckind instead of a raw s32 arg0, matching vi0102 usage with CKIND_MARIO and CKIND_LUIGI.

Added many MetroTRK C sources/headers and deleted corresponding asm files, including dispatch, dolphin_trk_glue, flush_cache, main_TRK, mainloop, mem_TRK_text, mpc_7xx_603e, msg, msgbuf, nubevent, nubinit, serpoll, targcont, and targimpl-related code.
Added src/metrotrk.h as an aggregate include for MetroTRK headers.
Updated build/compiler tooling to use compilers_20250520.zip, added MWCC_TRK_VERSION := 1.1p1, added CC_TRK, and routed src/MetroTRK/*.c.o through the TRK compiler version.
Touched config/GALE01/symbols.txt, config/GALE01/splits.txt, obj_files.mk, and many asm files, consistent with replacing asm with C and updating symbol/split layout.
smart_moves: Introduced a vi0102_ prefix and descriptive callback names, giving future vi files a clear naming pattern instead of only address-derived symbols.

Separated archive-loaded data into small local structs such as vi0102_archivejoints, vi0102_archivecamdata, vi0102_archivefogdata, and vi0102_data, making the .dat archive layout easier to reason about while matching existing HSD types.
Preserved struct-size evidence with STATIC_ASSERT after changing un_804D6F60_t field layout, reducing risk when converting inferred padding into named fields.
Used concrete engine/domain types where evidence allowed, for example GXColor for erase colors and CharacterKind for Player_80036E20.
Kept near-match work explicit in the PR body: remaining issues were identified as register swaps and allocations, which helps later contributors focus on compiler/codegen shape rather than semantics.
Added a separate MWCC_TRK_VERSION and per-directory CC override for MetroTRK, recognizing that MetroTRK likely needs a different compiler version than normal game code to match.
decomp_lessons: For vi files, prefer a file-prefixed naming scheme like vi0102_FunctionRole when the function role is known; keep address suffixes only where the role remains unknown.
Typed callback prototypes can propagate across neighboring vi files; changing one camera callback to accept an unused int revealed a reusable signature pattern for similar camera GX callbacks.
When replacing unknown byte arrays used as colors, GXColor can make intent clearer, but existing helpers expecting u8[4] may still require casts like (u8*) &erase_colors_vi0102.
Archive scene data is often easier to match when modeled as nested structs matching the asset table shape: joint data array, camera descriptor/animation pair, light data, and fog descriptor.
Character/setup helper prototypes should use domain enums such as CharacterKind once call sites prove the semantic type.
Large subsystem asm-to-C migrations may require compiler-version routing, not just source cleanup; MetroTRK was routed to MWCC 1.1p1 separately from the main MWCC 1.2.5 path.

Closed/unmerged PRs can still preserve useful naming and prototype evidence, but downstream reuse should verify against current master because no review comments or merge result validate the approach here.
naming_conventions: Use vi0102_ as the module prefix for Vi0102 functions.
Use descriptive callback names when roles are known: vi0102_JObjCallback, vi0102_CameraCallback, vi0102_RunFrame, vi0102_Initialize.
Use vi_RunCamera rather than vi_EraseScreen for the shared camera/display inline helper.
Use semantic parameter names and types such as CharacterKind ckind instead of s32 arg0 in player setup helpers.
For archive-backed local structs, names in the diff used lower-case module prefixes: vi0102_archivejoints, vi0102_camanims, vi0102_archivecamdata, vi0102_archivefogdata, vi0102_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted large MetroTRK asm files after adding corresponding C sources, a direct asm-to-C replacement tactic for dispatch, msgbuf, msghndlr, targimpl, and other TRK modules.
Added MWCC_TRK_VERSION := 1.1p1 and routed $(BUILD_DIR)/src/MetroTRK/%.c.o to CC_TRK, indicating compiler-version selection was part of the matching strategy.
The PR body says everything was a near match and that register swaps and allocations were the main remaining mismatch causes.
The diff retained small asm/data adjustments where needed, such as adding MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 bss storage in asm/MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.s.
Prototype cleanup was used as a matching aid: typed signatures in vi0102.h and player.h helped guide call shapes in other files.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
Because the PR was closed without merge, there is no recorded reviewer approval or rejection rationale in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that merged the vi0102 naming convention or renamed vi_EraseScreen to vi_RunCamera.
Check whether MetroTRK compiler routing to MWCC_TRK_VERSION 1.1p1 was accepted in a later PR.
Search current master for vi0102_Initialize and vi0102_CameraCallback to see which prototype choices survived.
Compare later un_804D6F60_t definitions against this PR's four s8 fields and size assertion.
Look for later review or merge history around PRs touching src/MetroTRK/targimpl.c and src/MetroTRK/msgbuf.c.
